sections.paragraph:
πρόειμι ptc. προών (εἰμί; cp. Il. 1, 70 τά τʼ ἐόντα τά τʼ ἐσσόμενα πρό τʼ ἐόντα; Hes., Theog. 32 τὰ τʼ ἐσσόμενα πρό τʼ ἐόντα and later wr.; pap, EpArist) be preexistent (Herm. Wr. 422, 25 Sc. ὁ προὼν θεός) of the Holy Spirit Hs 5, 6, 5.—Renehan ’82, 121.

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Il. Il. = Iliad, s. Hom.—List 5

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

  1. wr. = writers, writings

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Renehan Renehan = RR., Greek Lexicographical Notes—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 867
